rulings.Case Title: DEVINE HARDY VS. ALYCE CARDINALE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendants Anna Zharkova And Christine Shiba'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 2-7 And Requests For Documents, Set One, Number 1-9; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Anna Zharkova And Christine Shiba And Their Counsel, James Cook, In The Amout Of $523.05
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 2. PLAINTIFF DEVINE HARDY'S Motion For Protective Order Re: Defendants Anna Zharkova And Christine Shiba'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 2-7 And Requests For Documents, Set One, Number 1-9; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants Anna Zharkova And Christine Shiba And Their Counsel, James Cook, In The Amout Of $523.05. rulings.Case Title: DEVINE HARDY VS. ALYCE CARDINALE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Alyce Cardinale'S Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 2-7 And Requests For Documents, Set One, Number 1-6; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Alyce Cardinale And Her Counsel, Trevor Montgomery, In The Amount Of $1,430.05
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 2. PLAINTIFF DEVINE HARDY'S Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Alyce Cardinale's Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 2-7 And Requests For Documents, Set One, Number 1-6; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Alyce Cardinale And Her Counsel, Trevor Montgomery, In The Amount Of $1,430.05. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to (1) Special Interrogatories 3 and 6; Request for Production 2, but responses should be provided only for the time period between January 1, 2017 through May 1, 2017; and (2) Special Interrogatories 5 and 7 and Request for Documents 3-6. Motion is GRANTED as to Special Interrogatories 2 and 4. Request for sanctions is DENIED. =(501/GD)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 3. DEFENDANT AGNES KOZEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to April 13, 2018 on Court's own motion. =(501/GD)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY L. DAVIS VS. CARLSON YEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 4. DEFENDANTS CARLSON YEE, SHIRLEY LI, S AND C COMPANY LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. S and C Company, LLC's Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as all causes of action to allege facts in support of each cause of action giving rise to liability of this defendant. Demurrer by other defendants is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for violation of Consumer Legal Remedies Act. No opposition filed. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for quiet title for plaintiff to allege return of value received (equity) as Plaintiff alleges that the transaction, including deed is voidable, requiring Plaintiff to act equitably per Fleming v. Kagan (1961) 189 Cal.App.2d 791. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. All other causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Allegations for the purposes of demurrer must be taken as true. =(501/GD)

rulings.Case Title: ARLENE R. BROWN VS. LION SHARE INVESTMENTS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 5. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK N.A.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised by demurrer. No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file and serve amended complaint. =(501/GD)

rulings.Case Title: JEREMIAH TSAI VS. FERNANDO J TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 8, 2018, Line 9. PLAINTIFF JEREMIAH TSAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENIED. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Moving party failed to shift its burden and failed to provide evidence of compliance with SFRO 37.9(c). Neither the Complaint for Plaintiff's declaration state that Defendant was served with Form 1007. Motion for summary adjudication as to affirmative defenses of laches, estoppel and unclean hands is GRANTED. No substantive opposition filed. Motion for summary adjudication is otherwise denied. Triable issues of fact as to the breach of warranty of habitability, quiet enjoyment, retaliation, discrimination/failure to accommodate, and cancellation/waiver. =(501/GD)
